titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Ira Cohen Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday May 7, 2020 at 03:37

did.unittitle: Ira Cohen papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (206 boxes) + 14 broadside, 2 record album storage, and 1 art
did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, notebooks, photographs, artwork, objects, audiovisual materials, and electronic media the document the life and work of Ira Cohen. The papers include materials relating to other writers, artists, and groups including Ronnie Burk, Louise Landes-Levi, the Living Theatre, Gerard Malanga, Petra Vogt, Tom Weigel, various Beat generation writers, and others. Artwork includes mail art, paintings, drawings, screen prints, and posters. Objects include stamps, Cohen's typewriter, masks, and plaster and papier mache casts. Casts include life masks made by Cohen of friends including Allen Ginsberg and Julian Beck and a cast of William S. Burroughs' hand.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Johan Kugelberg on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2011.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 44, 93-97, 185-213 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 98, 214-219 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Ira Cohen Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ira Cohen Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Ira Cohen
bioghist.p: Ira Cohen (1935-2011) was an American poet, photographer, publisher, and filmmaker. He was associated with the Living Theatre, as well as various writers associated with the Beat generation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, notebooks, photographs, artwork, objects, audiovisual materials, and electronic media the document the life and work of Ira Cohen. The papers include materials relating to other writers, artists, and groups including Ronnie Burk, Louise Landes-Levi, the Living Theatre, Gerard Malanga, Petra Vogt, Tom Weigel, various Beat generation writers, and others. Artwork includes mail art, paintings, drawings, screen prints, and posters. Objects include stamps, Cohen's typewriter, masks, and plaster and papier mache casts. Casts include life masks made by Cohen of friends including Allen Ginsberg and Julian Beck and a cast of William S. Burroughs' hand.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eleven series: I. Selected Papers, 1924, 1960s-2000s. II. Correspondence, 1970s-2000s. III. Papers Relating to Other Writers and Groups, 1960s-2000s. IV. Manuscript Materials and Working Files, circa 1961-2007. V. Notebooks, 1979-2005. VI. Photographs, 1960s-2000s. VII. Papers Removed from Printed Material, undated. VIII. Artwork, 1970s-2000s. IX. Objects, circa 1936, 1980s-2000s. X. Audiovisual Materials, 1965-2000s. XI. Electronic Media, 1980s-2000s.

did.unittitle: Selected papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These materials were selected by Johan Kugelberg for use in a master class taught at the Beinecke Library in 2011.

Places include London, Brussels, New York, Crete, Erlangen. Also includes photographs by Ira Cohen, photographs of family and friends, objects, and events with music and costumes. Also includes some documents, artwork, cards, ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ira, The CD is just the audio of the video. The video is PAL system (European) so you will have to have it transferred to NTSC (US) to play it at home. The start of it is a bit messy as I was trying to adjust something in the camera at the time. It cuts and then it's a pretty smooth ride from there. Enjoy yourself watching and listening to yourself! Love, Benjamin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dear Ira, Forgive the delay - Reid (Argos' bro) made his exit a couple of weeks ago after a seven-month battle w/ melanoma. He was buried in a sheet on the land... still contemplative here - death is a mysterious affair!... Anyway, I send you your [?] 'Mama London': should you think worth 'netting' or whatever I can make SVHS copy (better quality). Much Love Ira, Corinna

Ira Cohen poet; Dharmaware Woodstock NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from White Buffalo Multi-Media dated 1999-09-14:

Dear Ira, We enjoyed your presence at Dharmaware as well as the opportunity to hear your poetry and see that amazing film - wow, what a rarity. Someone recently mentioned you'd like a copy of the video we shot that night, so here you go - unedited - no tripod, just a documentation we thought would fit in the Woodstock Museum archives. If you need any of it in any format, the original was done on DV format, so it's gigital and ready to go anywhere. Looking forward to your poetry again. Peace Brother, [?]

scopecontent.p: Brussels March 4, 2000

Dear Ira, So, finally, here is a VHS tape with the shots of the Duke Ellington Boulevard [and] La Casita scenes. As [?], it's in PAL system -I hope you'll enjoy it. I'm getting in touch with [?] Plump, to see what's possible for the event in June near Amsterdam. I'll let you know. I send you a lot of kisses, hoping to see you soon. Hugs, Geraldine Baar

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hi Ira! At last, some concrete news... Brussels falls down to eternity. On CD, your voice's always your voice Philippe's ever [non] me (so I should be a non-Philippe)...and at this time Gerard M is ever G. Malanga. The juice or several projects [makes] all grow, the most important thing after several others. Oh, Ira, don't fall asleep, and remember: is that your voice you hear when you're talking. Is that your talk you hear when you're thinking (about that)? Big friendship. Daniel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note with VHS tape dated Nov 6, 1994

Hello Ira, It's been awhile? Enclosed is "working" tape I use for [?][?] that is on-and-ongoing. Quality-control much improved since then (vis video) - put myself in college last few months - with a viewing to editing (and access to editing equipment) 2 wound up doing all sorts of preliminary courses on TV Commnication, Video Prod etc. Most intensive and I did well! Counteractive to the self-image of being tecnically inept. Shall we do more video one of these days? Love Corinna

Ayrev's Films: 1. The Black Fedora 2. Tire 3. Elvishead

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter with the videocassette 10/31

Raphael, I apologize for not getting this footage to you sooner. To be honest, it is [?] difficult to watch this film. The Black Fedora was [?][?] by Ayrev. We woke up at 5:30 a.m. on probably the [?] day of the year to shoot the film. I remember freezing our asses off that morning. This was the same assignment, but of my [?]. The assignment was continuous screen movement. It wasn't as cold that day, but we sure got some funny looks rolling this tire around the state house. [?] walking in and out of the building - personably [?] of some kind [?][?] of our way. [?] that we weren't supposed to be there. It was the first [?] of the [?] of a film camera - which, as you know, is not always [?]. ELVISHEAD was the biggie. For both of us it represents hundreds of hours of work. We gave up a [?] last semester to make this film happen. It was on the making of Elvishead that Ayrev and I [?] our film partnership which was more of a friendship. We had some great 4:00 in the morning and delirious [laughs?] making ELVISHEAD. Of course we wouldn't have given up all of our other friends, usually found our other loves, or spent the long hours if we didn't think the film was going to win a Noble Prize. At best we would be flown our to CANNES for it. Our delusions of grandeur transformed into true satisfaction that we had made a good first film and, above all, worked well together. So there are the [great] comments about [?] of the films. I have some other [?] footage of Ayrev that I took with a [?] Price pixel camera, but I don't have an adaptor to get it on VHS yet. You have the bulk of stuff though. Ayrev spoke extremely well of you. I have rarely heard someone speak of another friend with such respect. I mentioned that to you during the ceremony but I figured I'd say[?]. [?] have been in the process of [?] - or any change of circumstance demands. Ayrev [?] I had a lot of film [?] for the next six months so I have had to adjust. I hope to do a documentary, or the [?][?] underground in New York. I [?] to go to BALI and [?][?][?] documentary. That project is 85% going to happen right now. It needs a lot of planning. Raphael, I hope you are doing well right now. Let's keep in touch. Maybe we will both be at the same place some time soon. Either [?] of the [?], I am sure this something will work out - [Chad]

did.unittitle: Meguro (Tokyo Japan) reading and performance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Fine Arts Intl, Tokyo, 18 November 1988
Dear Ira,

I'm very sorry not to write you sooner. But during the summer season, and also the fall too, we were very busy. Because we have a restaurant in Karuizawa and the place is very famous for a summer resort. Roki and I, we had to stay and work there through the season.

How are you getting along these days? Suppose you are so busy [for] your work. And how was Kyoto? I was so happy to hear from you in Koyouto and we imagined that you have had a pretty good time there with Shakty.

We want to thank you for joining us in Meguro museum, because of you, we could have a nice time. I enclose the video tape which you want and wait. If you like it, I feel so happy. And John Solt kindly payed for it before he leaves from Tokyo. He said that he will back to the States on December. I'd like to see you again in Tokyo or in your country, and if you come to Tokyo again, please call me. Aoki also wants to send you his best and warmhearted regards. Best wishes and Loves, [?]

Flame's Follies created and edited by Flame Schon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes included after a screening:
'What I Didn't Say'
I wish I could be art-political, or anecdotal, but here's in hindsight approximately wht I might've, could've, should've said, were I not struck dumb...(I bit apocalyptical I guess)...

Intro remarks: I don't want to put any words or thoughts into your heads...so just relax and trust yourselves (trust me). ("turn off your mind, relax, and float downstream, it is not dying") OR: Simultaneously challenging and relaxing, FF2 is not for the rynaptically/neurologically uninitiated. It demands a degree of visual/artistic leteracay and requires, as well as paradoxically, enables, absolute attentiveness and utter surrender. You know who you are...

After video remarks: Perhaps you saw a veiled woman, dancing removing a veil, a bare breast, hands and feet and hand gestures, a glance at a watch, a mummer doing cartwheels, a deer running, fire and water and stone... perhaps you saw cyclical, repetitive, relentless images, or an electronic dance of light and color (fire and water and stone)...or perhaps you saw slow freighted imagery co-existing with fast intracellular frequencies, sbutle nuances within the interslices of endless changes of fire and water and stone...perhaps you saw trains and walking arrivals and departures...perhaps you boarded that train and went on a trip...or perhaps something reached deep inside you and caused a resnance of delight and attention...what you saw and what you felt and where you went was all intentional. Any further questions?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Description on Flame's Follies included:

Flame's Follies a woman in a veil calls into being images of railroads, stone sculptures, brilliant abstractions, fire and water, and dances them into a television devolution. Direct retinal delight pierces the veil of knowing. Flame Schon 1997, 25 min.

scopecontent.p: Letter included with the tape: Dated 1996-06-18

Ira, It was really nice getting together with John and others the other day. I hope to see you soon again. This is a video of TV program that my friend Rob Tranchin made on his deaf son for Texas TV station. I was very moved by this. I hope you find it interesting, too. Kyoko

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One of three letters/notes included with video.
Brussels, Monday night at Eric's with Gerard and Daniel:

Dear Ira, I've asked Eric to record for me your [plain] reading of 'Song to Nothing' that I will [put] into music (remember the [?] song your heard in Amsterdam last year?). He will bring with him a DAT machine if you want to add some other words (new poems) to be "recorded" by my [?], don't hesitate...if by any chance you could read also a text about 'Ethiopia' that could be very useful for Daniel's next video. You know about the slides I would need to [?] for a possible show in Belgium... I know you will be a wonderful guide for Eric and his charming manager. It would be wonderful if you 2 (3?) had a small gig together. Meanwhile, I'll be working for all of you. ENJOY THE VIDEOS!! Love, Philipe P.S. A big hi to Sky (Eric her address please) (Do you like your special advisor, Mr. Paradise Now?...) 'Czechmate' was a big success at the [?] premiere we organized [?] friend [?]...USE It.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Second of three letters included with tape:

Technic notice about the videotapes: * The "curfew tape" is [especially] for your readings, 2 time the video you know, with a 5 minute black space between. * The compiliation tape should may be like your apartment... Daniel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Third note (postcard) of three included with tape. Dated 1994-12-11 Envelope reads: The Akasaic Internationalists The Big G and the Great P and "the naughty bitch" says my dear neighbor

Dear Ira Having a wonderful time with Philippe. We're doing a dozens CDs, 7 movies with Daniel Mageon, a mini-series with TV Belgium a multi-book contract. Thanks for [?] contact. WHO! LOVES! YOU! Following with the Big G [?]. Working hard (how lucky I am) and commited to spread the unkown vs poets by [surreal] hallucinations and old European [?]. Feeling kind of good [exhilaration] I had with my [?] ventriloquist in NYC, almost family days with poppy "stars" and bondage waffles ladies, your smiling beard and good [rales] are with us. Take good care of the Saxo [?]. Enjoy the videos. Use them. Frienship and love are for free.Soon will be [?] u... MAD TINTIN

scopecontent.p: Word/Play performance program is included with the tape.

Ira Cohen's write-up is as follows: Ira Cohen's selected poems "On Feet of Gold", were published by Synergetic Press in 1986. His most recent book is "media Shaman", with Angus MacLise and Gerard Malanga. (Temple Press, England, 1991) He is the editor and publisher of the Akashic Bulletin. (6/7)
